title: Channel test failure under multi-socket fan-out
prompt: Write `docs/multi_socket_channel_debug.md` diagnosing: a channel test that joins 3 channels on the same UserSocket connection (`room:lobby`, `room:general`, `room:private`) fails with `DBConnection.OwnershipError` on the second `join/3`. The allow_ecto_sandbox call is correctly placed at the start of each join. Must explain: (1) a Phoenix socket can host multiple channel PROCESSES on a single connection, (2) each channel process is a SEPARATE pid and needs its own sandbox allowance, (3) the UserSocket's `connect/3` stores the metadata in socket.assigns, but each channel's join/3 re-reads that assign and calls allow/2 on the CURRENT channel process, (4) the test must subscribe_and_join each channel with a freshly-minted socket OR ensure the metadata in socket.assigns is correctly propagated to each new channel process. Provide a fix: use `Phoenix.Channel.Server.socket/1` helper or pass explicit metadata in join params.
tier_rationale: Multi-channel lifecycle + sandbox — rarely documented in one place.
